title: Simit Sesame Ring Bread

seo.title: Simit Recipe: Authentic Turkish Sesame Ring Bread
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best authentic Turkish Simit recipe at home! This sesame ring bread is perfect for breakfast or a snack. Get the easy step-by-step guide.
seo.h1: Authentic Simit Recipe: Turkish Sesame Ring Bread
h2Headings: What is Simit Sesame Ring Bread?
Pro Tips for the Best Simit Results
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Turkish Simit sesame ring bread, golden brown and sprinkled with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy warm, homemade Simit, the iconic Turkish sesame ring bread, perfect for dipping in cheese or jam.

description: Simit is a beloved Turkish circular bread, encrusted with sesame seeds and characterized by its delightful chewiness and golden-brown crust. Often enjoyed for breakfast or as a quick snack, it pairs wonderfully with cheese, olives, and tea. This iconic street food brings a taste of Turkey's vibrant culinary heritage right to your home.

ingredients.notes: or pomegranate molasses, or honey mixed with 1/4 cup water

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it is smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare Molasses Dip and Sesame Seeds
instructions.text: While the dough is rising, prepare the dipping station. In a shallow bowl, whisk together the grape molasses and 0.5 cup of water until well combined. In another shallow bowl, spread out the toasted sesame seeds evenly.

instructions.title: Shape the Simits
instructions.text: Punch down the risen dough and divide it into 8-10 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a long rope, about 18-20 inches (45-50 cm) long. Take two ropes, twist them together, and then join the ends to form a ring shape.

instructions.title: Dip and Coat Simits
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 400F (200C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Dip each simit ring first into the molasses mixture, ensuring it's fully coated, then immediately transfer it to the bowl of sesame seeds, pressing gently to ensure a generous coating on all sides.

instructions.title: Bake the Simits
instructions.text: Arrange the coated simits on the prepared baking sheet, leaving some space between them. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy. You may need to bake in batches depending on your oven size.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, transfer the simits to a wire rack to cool slightly. Simit is best enjoyed warm. Serve with Turkish tea, white cheese, olives, and fresh tomatoes for an authentic Turkish breakfast experience.

tips: For an authentic Turkish flavor, using genuine grape molasses (pekmez) is key. If unavailable, a mixture of honey and water can be a good substitute, though the flavor profile will be slightly different.
Ensure your sesame seeds are well-toasted before use for maximum flavor and aroma. You can lightly toast them in a dry pan over medium heat until fragrant and golden.
Simit is best enjoyed fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, they can be briefly reheated in a toaster oven to restore their crispiness and warmth.
article: Simit Sesame Ring Bread is a beloved Turkish Baking dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
